Matt.summary: Matt Gourley is a co-host and executive producer of the Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend podcast, working alongside Conan O'Brien and Sona Movsesian. A 6'5" Aquarius with nearly 30 years of media experience, Matt is known for his sharp comedic timing, absurdist humor, and ability to pivot seamlessly between serious and silly moments. Despite his tech anxiety and self-deprecating nature, he's a skilled improviser who brings quick quips and emotional intelligence to conversations. Outside the podcast, Matt is a father with a young child, an original TMNT comics collector, and an amateur mountain biker.
facts: Matt Gourley is co-host and executive producer of Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend podcast
He works with Conan O'Brien and Sona Movsesian on the show
Matt is 6 feet 5 inches tall and an Aquarius
He has nearly 30 years of experience in talk/media
Matt is deeply afraid of technology and has only used Zoom about seven times
He has a young child (nearly six months old) and a wife
Matt owns original Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles comics signed by Eastman and Laird
He previously worked at Disney theme parks, including as the voice of a talking trash can at Disneyland
advice: When joining a new D&D group, ensure you're playing the same game—it can be deadly serious or silly depending on the group
Stay calm but always be ready for any attack or bit (practice zanshin in comedy)
Look up and have more fun instead of staying too focused on work
Keep chocolate-covered almonds in your pocket to maintain energy levels
Don't change a thing if what you're doing is working
Walk tall and trust in your spirit
Engagement with content matters regardless of how you initially discovered it
stories: Matt encountered a bobcat while mountain biking in Sullivan Canyon, leading to an absurdist tale involving a six-hour fight and his death by heart attack
He was kicked out of a Dungeons and Dragons game group for not playing the same style as the rest of the group
Matt worked embarrassing jobs at Disney, including being a talking trash can and once having to chew his way out of a locker
He lost his composure when a guest showed up unexpectedly during a podcast episode and couldn't switch midstream
Matt claims to have tracked down three elite-taste people and sent out goons to find others
He tells stories about past lives as a union soldier killed by bayonets and rocks, and as a verbose farmer killed by other farmers
recurring_themes: Matt is a skilled improviser and comedian who makes sharp quips and deflects attention back to Conan
He's often teased by Conan and Sona but participates willingly in absurdist humor
Matt is uncomfortable with compliments and uses self-deprecation as a defense mechanism
He demonstrates emotional intelligence and picks up on subtle tones in conversations
Matt is characterized as both helping and undermining the show in equal measure
He's known for being ready to pivot in any comedic direction at any moment
Matt makes frequent sarcastic asides and derails conversations with tangential comments
Despite tech anxiety, he's technically proficient as the show's producer and engineer

Sona.summary: Sona Mosia is Conan O'Brien's longtime assistant (15+ years) and co-host/producer on the Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend podcast. An Armenian-Croatian woman raised Catholic, she's a New York Times bestselling author who speaks multiple languages and works from 1 PM onwards. She's married to Augie Takatisian and mother to twins Mikey and Charlie. Known for her unflinching humor, colorblindness, calm demeanor under pressure, and willingness to discuss taboo topics, Sona is the show's irreverent voice who fact-checks Conan, asks blunt questions, and brings out the funny in everyone. She's superstitious (evil eye charms), has a distinctive silhouette, wears glasses and arm tattoos, and maintains a detached, unbothered attitude toward work.
facts: Sona Mosia is Conan O'Brien's assistant and co-host/producer on his podcast for 15+ years
She's married to Augie Takatisian and has twins named Mikey and Charlie (nearly three years old)
Armenian-Croatian heritage, raised Catholic, speaks Armenian and multiple other languages
Colorblind (perceives red/green as brown/gray)
New York Times bestselling author who attended Ball State University for grad school
Works condom sizing specialist role (One Condom makes 52 different sizes)
Wears evil eye charms everywhere; has distinctive hairstyle, glasses, and arm tattoos
Learned English in her late 20s; didn't speak English when first hired by Conan
advice: Don't settle romantically—hold out for the right person
Appreciate all effort put into entertainment regardless of quality
Be calm and exude composure when dealing with anxious people
Risk taking bad first dates because they make good stories
Sex requires planning with kids—treat it like a coordinated military operation
Don't care too much about your job and still get paid
Be selective about what you preserve in life
Show urgency in marketing and advertising strategy
stories: Conan took Sona to Armenia twice; both times they were offered only dried pressed fruit for dessert
Conan's team called Sona a vampire for three years because her Armenian sounded like arguing with Dracula; she received vampire-themed Secret Santa gifts
Sona's mother treated her fractured arm with onions and crushed beans for two weeks before getting X-rays
Sona went to Jaipur, India with Conan for an American Express ad and loved the chaotic traffic energy
Sona's father from Brooklyn put a hex on Fabio after he attacked her mother at a show; Fabio was hit in the face by a goose a month later
She got too high on gummies at Disneyland and thought she was going into battle on Pirates of the Caribbean
Conan threw a box of tissues at Sona right before a podcast episode started
Sona coached brain surgeons and cardiac thoracic surgeons to care less about their jobs; they became happier
recurring_themes: Sona is always authentically herself—irreverent, unflinching, and unbothered by social norms
Dynamic with Conan involves her fact-checking, calling him an asshole, and controlling access to him
Vampire running joke—Conan constantly references Sona as vampire-adjacent due to her Armenian accent
Sona gets flustered around attractive men despite projecting confidence and detachment
She's portrayed as doing minimal actual work while being essential to the show's operation
Comfortable discussing graphic, taboo topics (sex, penis size, bodily functions) that make others squeamish
Conan publicly teases and makes fun of Sona to distract or put guests at ease, then forces reconciliation
Sona's calmness combined with constant comedic readiness—she practices 'zanshin' (alert but composed)

Conan.summary: Conan O'Brien is a 6'4" Irish-American comedian and television host with a distinctive pompadour and red hair who has spent 30+ years in entertainment. He hosts the podcast 'Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend' with producers Matt Gourley and Sona, and is known for his absurdist humor, willingness to be the butt of jokes, and deep knowledge of history and true crime. He's married with two children (Owen and Jackson), lives in Los Angeles with a second home in Seattle, and maintains a childlike approach to life and comedy.
facts: Hosts 'Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend' podcast with Matt Gourley and Sona
6'4" tall, red hair, distinctive pompadour, very pale
30+ year television career spanning SNL, The Simpsons, and late-night shows
Married with two children (Owen and Jackson); wife from Seattle/Montreal
Grew up in Brookline, Massachusetts; parents still live in original family home
Attended UC Santa Barbara
Plays Dungeons & Dragons every Saturday night with family
Lives in Los Angeles with second home in Seattle; loves Utah
advice: Dedicate yourself to absolute idiocy in pursuit of comedy
Talking with young, funny, nice people inspires hope about the future
Wrestling and performance should be fun, not work
You can't train unfunny people to be funny
People sometimes just want positivity and connection
Value family and children above professional achievements
Don't be afraid to be the butt of the joke yourself
Embrace absurdism and nonsense as part of regular life
stories: Lost his passport in London 2.5 hours before a flight
Got fired from Madison Square Garden tour guide job for telling visitors about a beautiful train station torn down to build the venue
Took a canoe trip in Maine where his legs were badly burned
Met Cher at age two in 1965 but has no memory of it
Was framed by his brother Justin and blamed for ruining his life
Scratches his back and occasionally his front on trees; learned not to use cedar
Did a 'Conan Must Go' trip to Norway where he was overshadowed by a pop duo of 10-year-olds at his hotel
Created luchador character 'the crazy rooster' in Mexico City without knowing wrestling was massive
recurring_themes: Self-deprecating humor and willingness to be ridiculed
Obsession with history, true crime, and Civil War
Physical comedy and cartoonish movements (Woody Woodpecker-like)
Creating nonsense words and babble ('gom gom,' 'Dom Oglebies')
Restlessness and foolishness (describes himself as easily fooled, didn't take logical life path)
Deep family bonds and time with children
Travel and exploring new places
Dismissing conventional wisdom (claims immunity to therapy and meditation due to Irish heritage)
